failure_modes.why: BLUE built only its starting two settlements and never expanded production, leaving it stuck at 2 VP all game.
BLUE laid almost no roads and built no new settlements after setup, getting completely boxed out of growth.
BLUE churned endless maritime trades (wheat/brick/sheep) without ever converting resources into settlements, cities, or dev cards.

review_summary: BLUE froze at its initial two settlements, never developed production or expanded, and burned its income on aimless maritime trades instead of building VP.

0.summary: Node 25 is a value-optimal opening settlement with zero oracle regret, scoring well across all placement criteria.

76.summary: Reasonable bank trade to grab scarce brick, but spending so heavily on 3:1 ratios while boxed in is inefficient.

123.summary: A defensible but low-value bank trade converting two bricks into one wheat with no immediate build payoff.

7.summary: A fine opening road with zero regret, only a slight question over which direction was optimal.

41.summary: A defensible 2:1 port trade for a needed wheat, but it doesn't enable an imminent build and drains brick with no clear plan.

failed.claude: One wood alone doesn't complete any build (no brick), so it only partially advances toward a settlement/road but is a reasonable step given boxed-in position.
failed.openai: The trade does not directly enable a key build, as wood alone does not advance towards any immediate construction.

209.summary: A defensible if low-value bank trade of surplus wheat for needed wood that doesn't immediately complete a build but doesn't help the opponent.

160.summary: A reasonable, regret-free road extension that maintains tempo and board control while leading the game.

329.summary: A solid, zero-regret road extension that maintains longest road and expansion potential while RED is comfortably ahead.

failed.claude: Getting a second wheat moves toward something, but with no clear imminent build, the trade doesn't complete anything meaningful this turn.
failed.openai: The trade does not advance towards a key build as there is no immediate plan that requires wheat.

304.summary: A defensible but low-impact 2:1 port trade that doesn't complete a build, with the oracle preferring to just hold the bricks.

252.summary: A sensible 3:1 maritime trade converting surplus sheep into needed wheat, matching the oracle's best move with zero regret.

364.summary: With no buildable option, buying a dev card is the strictly more productive choice over ending the turn.

392.summary: Buying the last dev card with leftover non-buildable resources is the clearly correct constructive play while leading.

431.summary: Building settlement 23 is a strong, zero-regret VP-gaining move for the already-leading RED.

454.summary: Robbing BLUE is the maximal-value robber placement, suppressing the opponent and stealing a card with no downside.

485.summary: Reasonable use of surplus brick via 3:1 port, though trading for wheat would have better progressed a city given the 2 ore on hand.
